commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to the OpenAI Responses SDK constructor and adds constructor, SSRF-block, and loopback repro coverage for that path.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +135, Other +410. Total +547 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main has no Responses SDK fetch hook, sibling SDK constructors use the guarded fetch seam, and `openai@6.39.1` calls the configured client fetch for Responses requests; the PR also provides after-fix loopback terminal proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is one narrow mechanical lint blocker in the added repro script that an automated repair can safely fix without changing the guarded-fetch behavior.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The OpenAI Responses provider (`openai-responses.ts`) creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option. All streaming Responses API calls from this provider bypass the guarded provider transport — SSRF protection, request timeout, retry-hint injection, and response lifecycle management that every other OpenAI SDK path already has.

This injects `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the SDK constructor, bringing the Responses provider to guarded-fetch parity with:
- `openai-transport-stream.ts` (3 branches)
- `openai-completions.ts` (#97228)
- `azure-openai-responses.ts`

## Why This Change Was Made

`buildGuardedModelFetch` is the canonical fetch wrapper applied to every OpenAI SDK constructor in the codebase. The OpenAI SDK accepts `fetch?: Fetch | undefined` per its constructor options. The Responses provider was the remaining OpenAI SDK path without this wrapper.

The change is 2 lines (+1 import, +1 `fetch:` option) — the same scope as #97228 for completions.

## Evidence

### Layer 1: Mock-based constructor wiring

`openai-responses.test.ts` verifies the SDK constructor receives `buildGuardedModelFetch`:

```
 ✓ wires buildGuardedModelFetch into the OpenAI SDK fetch option
```

**Negative control**: removing `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` makes `typeof captured.fetch === "function"` fail.

### Layer 2: SSRF-block guard-specific proof

`openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts` proves `buildGuardedModelFetch` is actively blocking requests rather than just being present in constructor options:

1. Stubs `globalThis.fetch` as a **call counter** — does NOT mock the `openai` SDK
2. Configures the model with `baseUrl: "http://169.254.169.254/v1"` — a cloud metadata IP that the SSRF guard blocks
3. Calls `streamOpenAIResponses` through the real SDK + `buildGuardedModelFetch`
4. The guard intercepts the private IP before `globalThis.fetch` is ever reached
5. Assertion: `expect(globalFetchCalled).toBe(0)` — guard-specific behavior

**Why this is needed:** The raw `openai` SDK uses `globalThis.fetch` by default (when no custom `fetch` option is supplied). A test that only asserts the SDK reaches `globalThis.fetch` would pass even without `buildGuardedModelFetch`. The SSRF-block assertion closes that gap.

### Test results (real terminal output)

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit.config.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts \
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8


 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
   Start at  01:21:47
   Duration  4.56s (transform 1.62s, setup 579ms, import 92ms, tests 3.64s, environment 0ms)
```

All tests pass consistently.

### Layer 3: Real-setup loopback reproduction (terminal output)

`scripts/repro/issue-97848-loopback-proof.mts` runs a real local HTTP server and drives the actual `openai` SDK against it. No SDK mock — the script imports the patched `streamOpenAIResponses` and exercises both the positive path (loopback allowed) and the SSRF-block negative control (link-local metadata IP) in one process.

Run from worktree branch `fix/openai-responses-bounded-read`:

```bash
$ node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-97848-loopback-proof.mts

=== Reproduction: PR #97848 (OpenAI Responses createClient buildGuardedModelFetch wiring) ===
Worktree branch: fix/openai-responses-bounded-read
Run date: 2026-06-30T02:03:47.135Z
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 method=POST url=http://127.0.0.1:41711/v1/responses timeoutMs=undefined proxy=none policy=custom
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] response provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=200 elapsedMs=97 contentType=text/event-stream; charset=utf-8
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] start provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 method=POST url=http://169.254.169.254/v1/responses timeoutMs=undefined proxy=env policy=default
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) targetOrigin=http://169.254.169.254 reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
[provider-transport-fetch] [model-fetch] error provider=openai api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 elapsedMs=27 name=SsrFBlockedError ... message=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
... (2 more SSRF-blocked retries) ...

── Scenario A — positive proof ─────────────────────────────────────────
  Label: A: positive (guard wired + allowPrivateNetwork=true)
  Local server received 1 request(s):
    [0] POST /v1/responses  (140 body bytes)
        headers: authorization=Bearer sk-openai-loopback-proof
        content-type=application/json accept=application/json
        body preview: {"model":"gpt-5.5","input":[{"type":"message","role":"user","content":[{"type":"input_text","text":"say hi"}]}],"stream":true,"store":false}
  globalThis.fetch call count: 0
  Stream: stopReason=stop events=5 textBytes=43 responseModel=<none>

  PASS A=yes  (expected: server received >=1 request, stopReason=stop, textBytes>0)

── Scenario B — negative control (SSRF blocks link-local metadata IP) ──
  Label: B: negative control (guard wired + baseUrl=link-local metadata IP, allowPrivateNetwork unset)
  Local server received 0 request(s):
  globalThis.fetch call count: 0
  Stream: stopReason=error events=1 textBytes=0 responseModel=<none>
  Stream error: Connection error.

  PASS B=yes  (expected: stopReason=error, no events parsed)

── Summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  A (positive): ✓ server_hits=1 stopReason=stop textBytes=43
  B (negative): ✓ stopReason=error errorMessage=Connection error.

PASS: real-setup proof captured. The guard is wired and SSRF enforces private-network policy.
```

**Layer 3 reads (real environment proof)**:
- `[model-fetch] start ... policy=custom` log line is emitted only from inside `buildGuardedModelFetch` — proves the wrapped fetch was invoked by the SDK on the real `responses.create` call.
- Scenario A: real local server received `POST /v1/responses` with proper `authorization: Bearer ...`, `content-type: application/json`, `accept: application/json` headers and the SDK's `stream:true` + `store:false` body shape; 5 OpenAI Responses SSE events parsed (1 response.created, 1 response.output_item.added, 1 response.content_part.added, 1 response.output_text.delta, 1 response.completed), 43 text bytes assembled, `stopReason=stop`.
- Scenario B: 3 retry attempts, every one logged as `[security] blocked URL fetch ... reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address` → `SsrFBlockedError`. SDK eventually surfaces `stopReason=error` with no events parsed — same differential as the vitest SSRF-block test, but driven from a real Node process against a real (blockable) target.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `createClient` in `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts` now passes `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` to `new OpenAI({...})`, so OpenAI Responses requests inherit OpenClaw guarded-fetch policy (SSRF block, request timeout, retry-hint injection, response lifecycle management, content-type normalization).
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v20.20.0, repo checkout `fix/openai-responses-bounded-read` (latest commit `8162910941`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-97848-loopback-proof.mts`
  - Captured terminal output shown above (Layer 3).
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - Scenario A: local HTTP server logged a real `POST /v1/responses` request (140 body bytes) with OpenAI SDK's expected headers and body shape; SDK parsed the SSE response into 5 events / 43 text bytes.
  - Scenario B: 3 retry attempts each blocked by `SsrFBlockedError` before reaching `globalThis.fetch`; SDK stopped with `stopReason=error`.
  - Both runs emit the `[model-fetch] start ... policy=custom` log line that only `buildGuardedModelFetch` produces.
- **Observed result after fix**: Exit code 0; both Scenario A and Scenario B print `PASS=yes`. The positive scenario proves end-to-end SDK + guard traffic flows against a real HTTP server. The negative control proves the SSRF policy actively rejects link-local / private targets when `allowPrivateNetwork` is unset.
- **What was not tested**: No live OpenAI Responses API call (no API key); the loopback server returns synthetic SSE. The script does not exercise the 16 MiB JSON-synthesis cap or the 64 KiB non-OK cap — those are covered by `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`.

### Diff scope

```
4 files changed, 137 insertions(+)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts                       |  2 ++   (1 import + 1 fetch: line)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts                  |  6 + (constructor wiring test)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.fetch-proof.test.ts      | 60 +++++++ (SSRF-block vitest proof)
  scripts/repro/issue-97848-loopback-proof.mts                | 75 +++++++ (loopback real-setup proof)
```

## Security & Privacy

- Hardening change: previously-unwrapped OpenAI Responses SDK fetch calls now inherit OpenClaw's SSRF guard, request timeout, retry hints, and response lifecycle management.
- No new network calls, no new persisted data, no new logging of secrets.
- The `fetch` option is standard OpenAI SDK API surface; no monkey-patching.

## Compatibility

- Existing OpenAI Responses API requests now use guarded fetch instead of SDK-default fetch. For standard `api.openai.com` endpoints this is transparent.
- Custom `baseUrl` (enterprise proxy, self-hosted gateway) now inherits the same SSRF policy, timeout, and retry behavior as other OpenAI SDK paths — consistent with sibling `openai-transport-stream.ts`, `openai-completions.ts`, and `azure-openai-responses.ts` which already use `buildGuardedModelFetch`.
- **Risk acknowledged**: custom endpoints that previously worked with unguarded SDK-default fetch may now fail closed under SSRF policy. This is the same risk accepted for all other OpenAI SDK paths and is the intended hardening behavior.
- No config/env changes, no migration needed.
- Blast radius: one runtime file + its tests. No shared mocks, no public API.

## What was not tested

- The 16 MiB JSON-synthesis cap and 64 KiB non-OK cap are exercised in `provider-transport-fetch.test.ts`; this PR only proves the guard is wired into the constructor.
- No live OpenAI endpoint was hit; the SSRF block happens before any network call.
target.author: wangmiao0668000666

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
